definition: Compute global or local alignment identity between evaluation and training sequences. Use this instead of fixed-position Hamming distance when sequences vary in length, contain indels, or need homology-aware train-eval separation.
example_usage: python alignment_identity_similarity.py --train train_sequences.csv --eval eval_sequences.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --sequence-col sequence

citations.title: GraphPart: homology partitioning for biological sequence analysis

evidence.summary: The paper argues that closely related sequence pairs crossing partitions overestimate predictive performance.
The homology partitioning problem is defined by maximum cross-partition sequence identity thresholds.
GraphPart evaluates maximum cross-partition pairwise identity and supports Needleman-Wunsch or MMseqs2 alignments.

limitations: The bundled example is a simple global alignment and is not a replacement for BLAST, MMseqs2, or domain-specific alignment pipelines.
Percent identity can miss remote homology and structural convergence.
Thresholds differ across proteins, DNA, RNA, and task families.
quality_gates: Report alignment method, scoring parameters, and whether local or global identity is used.
Use MMseqs2, BLAST, or HMM-based similarity for large or remote-homology datasets.
Plot the cross-split maximum identity distribution before interpreting the SPECTRA curve.
